content.text:
Now I can see the key method. Let me focus on the `CommitItemChangesAsync()` method (the parameterless version used for form editing mode), around lines 1977-2003. The issue is clear:

1. `CommittedItemChanges` is called first (line 1991) - at this point, changes have NOT been applied to the source item yet
2. Changes are applied to `_editingSourceItem` after the callback returns (lines 1997-1998)
3. The edit form is closed after that (line 2000-2001)

The fix should add an `AfterCommittedItemChanges` event that fires after changes are applied. Let me also check the `DataGridEditFormAction.cs` and look for existing tests.

11	{

13	    /// Close the edit form after <see cref="MudDataGrid{T}.CommittedItemChanges"/> has completed.
14	    /// </summary>
15	    Close,
16	
17	    /// <summary>
18	    /// Keep the edit form open after <see cref="MudDataGrid{T}.CommittedItemChanges"/> has completed.
19	    /// </summary>
20	    KeepOpen
21	}
22

<MudDataGrid T="Item" Items="@_items" EditMode="@DataGridEditMode.Cell"
             RowClick="@OnRowClick"
             RowContextMenuClick="@OnRowContextMenuClick"
             SelectedItemChanged="@OnSelectedItemChanged"
             SelectedItemsChanged="@OnSelectedItemsChanged"
             CommittedItemChanges="@OnCommittedItemChanges"
             StartedEditingItem="@OnStartedEditingItem"
             CanceledEditingItem="@OnCanceledEditingItem"
             ReadOnly="false">
    <Columns>

@code {
    public static string __description__ = "Used for Unit Tests to verify SelectedItem/SelectedItems Changed";

    private IEnumerable<Item> _items = new List<Item>()
    {
        new Item("A"),
        new Item("B"),
        new Item("C")
    };

    public bool RowClicked { get; set; }
    public bool RowContextMenuClicked { get; set; }
    public bool SelectedItemChanged { get; set; }
    public bool SelectedItemsChanged { get; set; }
    public bool CommittedItemChanges { get; set; }
    public bool StartedEditingItem { get; set; }
    public bool CanceledEditingItem { get; set; }

    private void OnRowClick(DataGridRowClickEventArgs<Item> args)
    {
        RowClicked = true;
    }

    private void OnRowContextMenuClick(DataGridRowClickEventArgs<Item> args)
    {
        RowContextMenuClicked = true;
    }

    private void OnSelectedItemChanged(Item item)
    {
        SelectedItemChanged = true;
    }

    private void OnSelectedItemsChanged(HashSet<Item> items)
    {
        SelectedItemsChanged = true;
    }

    private Task<DataGridEditFormAction> OnCommittedItemChanges(Item item)
    {
        CommittedItemChanges = true;
        return Task.FromResult(DataGridEditFormAction.Close);
    }

    private void OnStartedEditingItem(Item item)
    {
        StartedEditingItem = true;
    }

    private void OnCanceledEditingItem(Item item)
    {
        CanceledEditingItem = true;
    }

    public class Item
    {
        public string Name { get; set; }

        public Item(string name)
        {
            Name = name;
        }
    }

}

Let me also check the `DataGridExtensions.cs` since that may be relevant:

2. Fire it after changes are applied to `_editingSourceItem` in the `CommitItemChangesAsync()` method

content.content: The file /testbed/src/MudBlazor/Components/DataGrid/MudDataGrid.razor.cs has been updated successfully.

lines: -        /// Invoked when the user saves changes to an item, allowing for validation,
+        /// Invoked when the user saves changes to an item, allowing for validation,
-        /// The callback receives a separate instance of the item with the committed values,
-        /// preventing changes from being applied to the underlying data source before
+        /// The callback receives a separate instance of the item with the committed values,
+        /// preventing changes from being applied to the underlying data source before
-        /// Return a <see cref="DataGridEditFormAction"/> value to control the edit form behavior.
+        /// Return a <see cref="DataGridEditFormAction"/> value to control the edit form behavior.
public Func<T, Task<DataGridEditFormAction>>? CommittedItemChanges { get; set; }
+        /// Occurs after changes have been committed and applied to the underlying item.
+        /// This event fires after edits have been successfully applied to the bound data source,
+        /// making it the appropriate place to perform actions like equality comparisons or saving
+        /// that depend on the final state of the item.
+        /// Unlike <see cref="CommittedItemChanges"/>, which receives a copy of the item before
+        /// changes are applied, this event receives the source item after changes have been applied.
+        /// This event only fires when <see cref="EditMode"/> is <see cref="DataGridEditMode.Form"/>.
/// Occurs when a field changes in the edit dialog.

343	        /// </para>

345	        /// <item><see cref="DataGridEditFormAction.Close"/> - Accepts changes and closes the form</item>

Let me simplify the test by removing the need for MudForm. The key approach is to directly set the internal fields and call CommitItemChangesAsync. The simplest way to handle MudForm validation is to set `_valid` via reflection since it's private. Let me rewrite the test cleanly:

<MudDataGrid T="Item" Items="@_items" EditMode="@DataGridEditMode.Form"
             CommittedItemChanges="@OnCommittedItemChanges"
             CommittedItemChanged="@OnCommittedItemChanged"
             ReadOnly="false"
             @ref="_dataGrid">
    <Columns>
        <PropertyColumn Property="x => x.Name" />
    </Columns>
</MudDataGrid>

@code {
    public static string __description__ = "Used for Unit Tests to verify the CommittedItemChanged event fires after changes are applied";

    private IEnumerable<Item> _items = new List<Item>()
    {
        new Item("A"),
        new Item("B"),
        new Item("C")
    };

    public MudDataGrid<Item> _dataGrid;

    public bool CommittedItemChangesCalled { get; set; }
    public bool CommittedItemChangedCalled { get; set; }
    public string? CommittedItemChangedName { get; set; }

    private Task<DataGridEditFormAction> OnCommittedItemChanges(Item item)
    {
        CommittedItemChangesCalled = true;
        return Task.FromResult(DataGridEditFormAction.Close);
    }

    private void OnCommittedItemChanged(Item item)
    {
        CommittedItemChangedCalled = true;
        CommittedItemChangedName = item.Name;
    }

    public class Item
    {
        public string Name { get; set; }

        public Item(string name)
        {
            Name = name;
        }
    }
}

content.text:
Two issues:
1. `_items` is private in the test component
2. `IsValid` on MudForm is a `[Parameter]` and shouldn't be set externally with `BL0005`

Let me fix the test component to make `_items` accessible and fix the test approach:

file.content:
<MudDataGrid T="Item" Items="@_items" EditMode="@DataGridEditMode.Form"
             CommittedItemChanges="@OnCommittedItemChanges"
             CommittedItemChanged="@OnCommittedItemChanged"
             ReadOnly="false"
             @ref="_dataGrid">
    <Columns>
        <PropertyColumn Property="x => x.Name" />
    </Columns>
</MudDataGrid>

@code {
    public static string __description__ = "Used for Unit Tests to verify the CommittedItemChanged event fires after changes are applied";

        new Item("B"),
        new Item("C")

        CommittedItemChangedName = item.Name;
    }

    public class Item
    {
        public string Name { get; set; }

        public Item(string name)
        {
            Name = name;
        }
    }
}
